title: A priori estimate of gradient of a solution to certain differential   inequality and quasiconformal mappings
summary:
We will prove a global estimate for the gradient of the solution to the {\it
Poisson differential inequality} $|\Delta u(x)|\le a|\nabla u(x)|^2+b$, $x\in
B^{n}$, where $a,b<\infty$ and $u|_{S^{n-1}}\in C^{1,\alpha}(S^{n-1}, \Bbb
R^m)$. If $m=1$ and $a\le (n+1)/(|u|_\infty4n\sqrt n)$, then $|\nabla u| $ is a
priori bounded. This generalizes some similar results due to E. Heinz
(\cite{EH}) and Bernstein (\cite{BS}) for the plane. An application of these
results yields the theorem, which is the main result of the paper: A
quasiconformal mapping of the unit ball onto a domain with $C^2$ smooth
boundary, satisfying the Poisson differential inequality, is Lipschitz
continuous. This extends some results of the author, Mateljevi\'c and
Pavlovi\'c from the complex plane to the space.

title: On the Korteweg-de Vries long-wave approximation of the Gross-Pitaevskii   equation II
comment:
Final version accepted for publication in Communications in Partial
  Differential Equations with a few minor corrections and added remarks
summary:
In this paper, we proceed along our analysis of the Korteweg-de Vries
approximation of the Gross-Pitaevskii equation initiated in a previous paper.
At the long-wave limit, we establish that solutions of small amplitude to the
one-dimensional Gross-Pitaevskii equation split into two waves with opposite
constant speeds $\pm \sqrt{2}$, each of which are solutions to a Korteweg-de
Vries equation. We also compute an estimate of the error term which is somewhat
optimal as long as travelling waves are considered. At the cost of higher
regularity of the initial data, this improves our previous estimate.

title: Amortissement Landau
comment:
10 pages -- version fran\c{c}aise de la note "Landau damping"
  hal-00383920
summary:
Dans cette note nous pr\'esentons les principaux r\'esultats du r\'ecent
travail hal-00376547/arXiv:0904.2760, o\`u le ph\'enom\`ene d'amortissement
Landau est pour la premi\`ere fois \'etabli dans un contexte non lin\'eaire.
  -----
  In this note we present the main results from the recent work hal-00376547 /
arXiv:0904.2760, which for the first time establish Landau damping in a
nonlinear context.

title: Reflected BSDEs and continuous solutions of parabolic obstacle problem   for semilinear PDEs in divergence form
comment:
36 pages, 33rd Conference on Stochastic Processes and Their
  Applications, Berlin 2009
summary:
We consider the Cauchy problem for semilinear parabolic equation in
divergence form with obstacle. We show that under natural conditions on the
right-hand side of the eqution and mild conditions on the obstacle a unique
continuous solution of the problem admits a stochastic representation in terms
of reflected backward stochastic differential equations. We derive also some
regularity properties of so- lutions and prove useful approximation results.

title: Waves, damped wave and observation
comment:
6 figures, French-Chinese Summer Institute on Applied Mathematics,
  references are updated
summary:
We consider the wave equation in a bounded domain (eventually convex). Two
kinds of inequality are described when occurs trapped ray. Applications to
control theory are given. First, we link such kind of estimate with the damped
wave equation and its decay rate. Next, we describe the design of an
approximate control function by an iterative time reversal method.
